get.summary: Gets the current status of the Fallen-8 database

200.description: Returns the database status information

head.summary: Trims the database, releasing unused memory

put.summary: Loads a Fallen-8 database from a saved file

requestBody.description: Load specification including file path and service start options

400.description: Invalid load specification or file not found

500.description: The load transaction was rolled back and did not complete

put.summary: Saves the current database state to a file
put.description:
Sample request:

    PUT /save
    {
       "saveGameLocation": "C:/Fallen8/database.f8s",
       "savePartitions": 8
    }

Both parameters are optional. If not provided, defaults to the base directory with filename "Temp.f8s" and optimal partition count.
The save is recorded in the save-game registry (feature save-games); the response is the
created entry, whose "location" field is the path the database was saved to.

requestBody.description: Save specification including file path and partition options (both optional)

200.description: Returns the created save-game registry entry

400.description: Invalid save specification

500.description: The save transaction was rolled back and did not complete

head.summary: Clears all data from the database (resets to empty state)

get.summary: Gets the total number of vertices in the database

200.description: Returns the number of vertices

get.summary: Gets the total number of edges in the database

200.description: Returns the number of edges

post.summary: Creates a new service based on the specified plugin

requestBody.description: Plugin specification including type, ID and options

200.description: Returns whether the service was successfully created

400.description: Invalid plugin specification

delete.summary: Deletes a service with the specified key

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the service to delete

200.description: Returns whether the service was successfully deleted

put.summary: Uploads and registers a new plugin to the database
put.description:
SECURITY: an uploaded plugin is loaded and executed IN-PROCESS WITH FULL TRUST - a trust
boundary, not a sandbox. Requires an authenticated caller AND
Fallen8:Security:EnableDynamicPluginLoading=true. The DLL is written to the configured,
isolated plugin directory, never next to the server binaries.

requestBody.description: The plugin DLL binary content as a stream

400.description: Invalid plugin data or incompatible plugin

403.description: Dynamic plugin loading is disabled on this server (Fallen8:Security:EnableDynamicPluginLoading)

get.summary: Lists the discovered analytics algorithm plugins

200.description: The available algorithms with their descriptions

post.summary: Runs an analytics algorithm over the graph
post.description:
Runs synchronously under the wall-clock budget (default 30 s). Score algorithms
return the top-K vertices by score (descending, ascending id tie-break); partition
algorithms return partition summaries (largest first). The FULL per-vertex result's
delivery vehicle is the opt-in property write-back ("writeBack": true), which lands
through chunked plugin write transactions; write-back durability is SNAPSHOT-ONLY -
a WAL-only replay with no intervening save loses the written properties (re-run to
restore, overwrite is idempotent).

Reaching the iteration cap is a NORMAL 200 (converged=false, values usable), as is
budget exhaustion after at least one completed pass (budgetExhausted=true).

Sample request:

    POST /analytics/PAGERANK
    {
       "vertexLabel": "person",
       "maxResults": 10,
       "parameters": { "DampingFactor": 0.85 }
    }

parameters.name: algorithmName
parameters.description: The plugin name (e.g. PAGERANK, WCC, LABELPROPAGATION, DEGREE, TRIANGLECOUNT)

requestBody.description: Scoping, budgets, algorithm parameters, result bound and the optional write-back

200.description: The run's result (including converged=false / budgetExhausted=true partials that carry usable values)

400.description: Unknown direction/parameter values, out-of-ceiling maxIterations/maxResults/timeBudgetSeconds, or a bad write-back property key

404.description: Unknown algorithm name

408.description: The wall-clock budget exhausted with no usable result

post.summary: Returns one partition's membership page from a fresh run of a partition algorithm
post.description:
Analytics runs are one-shot (no job store), so the page comes from a FRESH run with
the same specification - deterministic for a quiescent graph. Use offset+maxResults
to page; the page ceiling is 10000 rows.

parameters.name: algorithmName
parameters.description: A partition algorithm (WCC or LABELPROPAGATION)

parameters.name: partitionId
parameters.description: The partition id from a previous run's summaries

requestBody.description: The same run specification, plus the page offset

200.description: The membership page

400.description: Invalid specification, a negative offset, writeBack (not supported here), or a score algorithm

404.description: Unknown algorithm or a partition id the run did not produce (including an empty scope)

408.description: The wall-clock budget exhausted with no usable result

200.description: OK

get.summary: Runs the edge-traversal benchmark and returns structured statistics

parameters.name: iterations
parameters.description: Number of timed iterations (default 1000)

200.description: The benchmark statistics

400.description: Empty graph, non-positive or non-numeric iteration count

get.summary: Streams the graph (or a label-filtered subset) as newline-delimited JSON.
get.description:
The stream is fallen8-jsonl version 1: one meta line (format version + exact counts),
then vertex lines, then edge lines. Edges whose endpoints are not both in the exported
vertex set are omitted, so EVERY exported file is internally consistent and importable
by construction.

CONSISTENCY (honest): this is data interchange, not a crash-consistent backup. Reads
are lock-free; a write committed during the export may or may not appear. The
guarantee is internal consistency plus "everything committed before the export began
is present" (subject to the label filters). For a point-in-time backup, quiesce writes
or use the save-game machinery. The same contract covers a property that an
embedded/plugin writer adds DURING the stream: if it is not exportable it is omitted
from its element rather than aborting the response (the REST write path cannot create
such properties; the pre-stream 422 covers everything present at capture).

parameters.name: vertexLabel
parameters.description: Optional: export only vertices with exactly this label

parameters.name: edgeLabel
parameters.description: Optional: export only edges with exactly this label

200.description: The NDJSON stream (application/x-ndjson)

422.description:
An element carries a property outside the exportable type
     allow-list (or a null value); the body names the element and property. Sent BEFORE any
     streaming, so a failed export is never a half-written file

post.summary: Imports a fallen8-jsonl stream into an EMPTY graph, assigning fresh engine ids.
post.description:
The body is read as a stream and processed line by line; lines batch into large
create transactions (Fallen8:BulkIO:ImportBatchSize per transaction = one WAL entry +
one fsync each). File ids are remapped unconditionally: they are references within the
file, resolved for edge endpoints, never preserved as engine ids. A leading meta line
is optional (grep-filtered subset files stay valid); when present, its counts act as a
truncation guard.

FAIL-FAST (honest): the first invalid line aborts the import with its exact line
number. Batches committed before the failure STAY COMMITTED (each batch is atomic and
WAL-logged; the file is not one transaction) - the error body reports the committed
counts, and because import requires an empty graph, recovery is always "/tabularasa,
fix the line, retry".

NOTE on the body cap: when Fallen8:BulkIO:MaxImportRequestBytes is configured, a real
Kestrel host may enforce it at the transport layer and answer 413 before this action's
own check runs - the status is the same, but the problem body with committed counts is
only guaranteed when the application-level check fires first.

200.description: The import completed; the body carries created counts

400.description:
A line was invalid (malformed JSON, unknown fields, bad property
     type/value, duplicate file id, unresolved edge endpoint, over-long line, meta-count
     mismatch) - the problem body carries lineNumber and the committed counts

409.description:
The graph is not empty (import requires an empty target; use
     /tabularasa or a fresh instance)

413.description: The request body exceeds the configured Fallen8:BulkIO:MaxImportRequestBytes

500.description: A batch transaction faulted internally; committed counts are reported

get.summary: Streams committed graph mutations as Server-Sent Events, with declarative server-side filtering and catch-up.

parameters.name: kinds
parameters.description: Event kinds to include (repeatable or comma-separated): vertexCreated, vertexRemoved, edgeCreated, edgeRemoved, propertySet, propertyRemoved. Unset = all kinds. resync events always pass.

parameters.name: elements
parameters.description: Element types to include: vertex, edge. Unset = both.

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: Element labels to include (exact, case-sensitive). Unset = any label. An unlabeled element never matches a labels filter.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Property keys to include (exact, case-sensitive). Only property events carry a key, so setting this excludes create/remove events.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Catch-up position: the last seen SSE id ("<epoch>:<seq>") or a bare sequence number. Buffered missed events replay first; a position outside the buffered window (or from another process epoch) starts the stream with resync(seekOutOfRange). The Last-Event-ID header (native EventSource reconnect) is honoured when this parameter is unset.

200.description: The SSE stream (text/event-stream); it stays open until the client disconnects

400.description: An unknown kind/element value or a malformed since position

503.description: The change feed is disabled (Fallen8:ChangeFeed:Enabled=false) or the concurrent subscriber limit (Fallen8:ChangeFeed:MaxSubscribers) is reached

post.summary: Validates a single delegate fragment without executing it
post.description:
The fragment is wrapped and compiled exactly as the path (POST /path/{from}/to/{to})
and subgraph (PUT /subgraph) endpoints would wrap it, but nothing is emitted, loaded,
or executed - validation is side-effect free.

Diagnostic positions are already mapped back to the submitted fragment (1-based; line 1
is the fragment's first line), so an editor renders markers without further mapping.
A null/empty fragment is valid by definition (it means "match everything" / "no custom
cost"). Warnings are reported but do not make the fragment invalid.

The endpoint sits behind the same dynamic-code authorization gate as the query
endpoints: compilation is the expensive half of that surface, and validation is only
useful where fragment submission is possible at all.

Sample request:

    POST /delegates/validate
    {
       "delegateKind": "VertexFilter",
       "fragment": "return (v) => v.TryGetProperty(out int age, \"age\") && age > 30;"
    }

requestBody.description: The delegate kind and the fragment to compile-check

200.description: The validation result (also for invalid fragments - inspect "valid")

400.description: Unknown delegateKind or malformed request

403.description: Dynamic code execution is disabled on this server (Fallen8:Security:EnableDynamicCodeExecution)

429.description: Rate limit for sensitive endpoints exceeded

post.summary: Embeds a text and stores it as the element's named embedding
post.description:
The generated vector is written through the element-embeddings surface together
with the provider's model-identity stamp (one atomic transaction); a vector index
bound to the name updates its projection on commit.

requestBody.description: Aborts the provider call when the request is cancelled

200.description: The embedding was generated and committed

400.description: Missing/oversized text or an invalid embedding name

404.description: The graph element does not exist

409.description: The provider's dimension or model identity conflicts with a vector index bound to this embedding name

502.description: The embedding backend produced invalid output

503.description: The embedding backend is unavailable (failed to load, or the Ollama sidecar is down)

post.summary: Embeds a batch of texts onto elements - one provider batch, one transaction
post.description: The bulk-ingestion path: every vector plus its model stamp commits atomically.

requestBody.description: Aborts the provider call when the request is cancelled

200.description: The batch was generated and committed

400.description: Empty/oversized batch, missing text, or an invalid embedding name

404.description: A referenced graph element does not exist

409.description: The provider's dimension or model identity conflicts with a bound vector index

502.description: The embedding backend produced invalid output

post.summary: Semantic search: embeds a query text and runs kNN against a vector index
post.description:
The text is embedded ONCE (with the configured query prefix); scores and ordering
are exactly those of POST /scan/index/vector for the same vector.

requestBody.description: Aborts the provider call when the request is cancelled

200.description: The hits, best first, with raw scores

400.description: Missing text, invalid k/kind, or the index is not a vector index

404.description: No index with the given name exists

409.description: The index's dimension or declared model identity conflicts with the active provider

502.description: The embedding backend produced invalid output

post.summary: Embeds raw texts and returns the vectors
post.description:
For clients driving the raw vector surfaces themselves (external pipelines,
    semantic path queries with a client-held vector, debugging).

requestBody.description: Aborts the provider call when the request is cancelled

200.description: The vectors, in input order, plus the model identity

400.description: Empty/oversized batch or a missing text

429.description: The sensitive-endpoint rate limit was exceeded

502.description: The embedding backend produced invalid output

put.summary: Creates a new vertex in the graph

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

requestBody.description: The vertex specification containing label and property information

202.description: Vertex creation accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Invalid vertex specification

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

put.summary: Creates a new edge between two vertices in the graph

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

requestBody.description: The edge specification containing source, target and property information

202.description: Edge creation accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Invalid edge specification

404.description: A referenced source or target vertex does not exist (only when waitForCompletion is true)

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

get.summary: Retrieves a graph element (vertex or edge) by its identifier

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element to retrieve

200.description: Returns the graph element object

204.description: Graph element with the specified ID was not found

delete.summary: Removes a graph element (vertex or edge) from the graph

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element to remove

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

202.description: Graph element removal accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Malformed request (e.g. a non-integer element id). (A non-existent graph element is NOT a 400: an out-of-range id → 500, an in-range/absent id is a no-op → 202.)

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

get.summary: Retrieves the complete graph data including vertices and edges

parameters.name: maxElements
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements to return (default: 1000)

200.description: Returns the graph data with vertices and edges

get.summary: Gets the source vertex ID for a specific edge

parameters.name: edgeIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the edge

200.description: Returns the source vertex ID

404.description: Edge with the specified ID was not found

get.summary: Gets the target vertex ID for a specific edge

parameters.name: edgeIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the edge

200.description: Returns the target vertex ID

404.description: Edge with the specified ID was not found

get.summary: Gets all available outgoing edge property IDs for a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

200.description: Returns the list of outgoing edge property IDs

204.description: Vertex has no outgoing edges or vertex not found

get.summary: Gets all available incoming edge property IDs for a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

200.description: Returns the list of incoming edge property IDs

204.description: Vertex has no incoming edges or vertex not found

get.summary: Gets outgoing edges of a specific type from a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

parameters.name: edgePropertyIdentifier
parameters.description: The edge property identifier/type to filter by

200.description: Returns the list of matching outgoing edge IDs

204.description: No matching edges found or vertex not found

get.summary: Gets incoming edges of a specific type to a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

parameters.name: edgePropertyIdentifier
parameters.description: The edge property identifier/type to filter by

200.description: Returns the list of matching incoming edge IDs

204.description: No matching edges found or vertex not found

post.summary: Scans the graph for elements with a specific property value

parameters.name: propertyId
parameters.description: The property ID to scan for

requestBody.description: Scan specification with comparison operator and value

200.description: Returns the matching element IDs

400.description: Invalid scan specification

post.summary: Performs a scan operation on an index with a specific value and operator

requestBody.description: Index scan specification with index ID, operator and value

200.description: Returns the matching element IDs

400.description: Invalid scan specification or index not found

post.summary: Performs a range-based scan on an index between two values

requestBody.description: Range scan specification with index ID, limits and include/exclude options

200.description: Returns the matching element IDs within the range

400.description: Invalid range specification or index not found

post.summary: Performs a fulltext search on an indexed property

requestBody.description: Fulltext search specification with index ID and search terms

200.description: Returns the search results with highlighting

400.description: Invalid search specification or index not found

404.description: Index not found or is not a fulltext index

put.summary: Adds (or replaces) an element's embedding vector in a vector index
put.description:
One vector per element: adding again replaces. The generic PUT /index/{indexId} add
path cannot express a float[] key, which is why the vector family has this typed
endpoint (like fulltext and spatial have theirs).

Sample request (explicit mode):

    PUT /index/vector/myEmbeddings
    {
       "graphElementId": 42,
       "vector": [0.12, -0.5, 0.33]
    }

Sample request (property mode - reads the element's float[] property):

    PUT /index/vector/myEmbeddings
    {
       "graphElementId": 42,
       "propertyId": "embedding"
    }

parameters.name: indexId
parameters.description: The ID of the vector index

requestBody.description: The element and its vector - explicit ("vector") or read from a float[] property ("propertyId")

200.description: The vector was indexed (add-again replaced the previous vector)

400.description: Not a vector index, neither/both modes supplied, wrong dimension, NaN/Infinity components, zero-norm vector under Cosine, or the named property is missing / not a float[]

404.description: The index or the graph element does not exist

put.summary: Sets (or replaces) a named embedding on a graph element
put.description:
The element is the source of truth for its embedding (feature element-embeddings):
the write is WAL-durable element state, and every vector index BOUND to this
embedding name updates its projection on commit - no separate index add. Replace
semantics: one current vector per name.

Sample request:

    PUT /graphelement/42/embedding/default
    { "vector": [0.12, -0.5, 0.33] }

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element

parameters.name: embeddingName
parameters.description: The embedding name (letters, digits, '_', '-'; max 64 chars)

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

requestBody.description: The embedding vector

202.description: Embedding write accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Invalid embedding name, missing/empty vector, non-finite components, a dimension conflicting with a vector index bound to this name, or a zero-norm vector while a bound Cosine index exists

404.description: The graph element does not exist

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

delete.summary: Removes a named embedding from a graph element
delete.description:
Bound vector indices purge the element's projection on commit. Removing an absent
embedding is a committed no-op, matching the property surface.

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element

parameters.name: embeddingName
parameters.description: The embedding name

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

202.description: Embedding removal accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Invalid embedding name

404.description: The graph element does not exist

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

get.summary: Gets a named embedding of a graph element

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element

parameters.name: embeddingName
parameters.description: The embedding name

200.description: The stored embedding

400.description: Invalid embedding name

404.description: The graph element does not exist or carries no embedding of that name

post.summary: Finds the k nearest neighbours of a query vector in a vector index
post.description:
Exact brute-force kNN (SIMD): deterministic ordering - best score first, ties broken
by ascending element id. Constraints are applied BEFORE scoring, so the returned k are
k MATCHING elements. Removed elements never appear. The GraphRAG recipe: feed the
returned element ids into the existing traversal surface (POST /path, PUT /subgraph,
property reads) - similarity search lands ON the graph.

Sample request:

    POST /scan/index/vector
    {
       "indexId": "myEmbeddings",
       "query": [0.1, 0.2, 0.3],
       "k": 10,
       "kind": "vertex",
       "label": "person"
    }

requestBody.description: The kNN query: index, query vector, k, optional kind/label constraints

200.description: Returns the k best-scoring matching elements (fewer when the corpus is smaller)

400.description: Not a vector index, wrong query dimension, NaN/Infinity components, k outside [1, 1024], zero-norm query under Cosine, or an unknown kind value

404.description: The index does not exist

post.summary: Performs a spatial distance search using a spatial index

requestBody.description: Spatial search specification with index ID, reference element and distance

200.description: Returns the element IDs within the specified distance

400.description: Invalid search specification

404.description: Index not found, is not a spatial index, or reference element not found

put.summary: Adds or updates a property on a graph element (vertex or edge)

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element

parameters.name: propertyIdString
parameters.description: The ID/key of the property

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

requestBody.description: Property value specification

202.description: Property addition accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Malformed request body / invalid property specification. (A non-existent graph element is NOT a 400: an out-of-range id rolls back with an internal error → 500, and an in-range/absent id is a no-op → 202.)

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

delete.summary: Removes a property from a graph element (vertex or edge)

parameters.name: graphElementIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the graph element

parameters.name: propertyIdString
parameters.description: The ID/key of the property to remove

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: When true, waits for the transaction to complete before responding

202.description: Property removal accepted (and committed when waitForCompletion is true)

400.description: Malformed request (e.g. a non-integer element id). (A non-existent element/property is NOT a 400: an out-of-range id → 500, an in-range/absent id is a no-op → 202.)

500.description: The transaction was rolled back with an internal error (only when waitForCompletion is true)

get.summary: Gets the total count of incoming edges for a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

200.description: Returns the count of incoming edges

404.description: Vertex with the specified ID was not found

get.summary: Gets the total count of outgoing edges for a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

200.description: Returns the count of outgoing edges

404.description: Vertex with the specified ID was not found

get.summary: Gets the count of incoming edges of a specific type for a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

parameters.name: edgePropertyIdentifier
parameters.description: The edge property identifier/type to count

200.description: Returns the count of matching incoming edges (0 if the vertex has no such edge group)

404.description: Vertex with the specified ID was not found

get.summary: Gets the count of outgoing edges of a specific type from a vertex

parameters.name: vertexIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the vertex

parameters.name: edgePropertyIdentifier
parameters.description: The edge property identifier/type to count

200.description: Returns the count of matching outgoing edges (0 if the vertex has no such edge group)

404.description: Vertex with the specified ID was not found

post.summary: Calculates the shortest path between two vertices in the graph
post.description:
The path specification allows for dynamic filtering and cost calculation using compiled C# code fragments.

Select the algorithm via "pathAlgorithmName". "BLS" is a hop-count (unweighted) shortest
path that ignores the "cost" block ("totalWeight" is 0), whereas "DIJKSTRA" is a weighted
shortest path that honours the "cost" block and "maxPathWeight" (and returns the K
least-weight loop-free paths when "maxResults" > 1).

IMPORTANT: Filter and cost properties must contain valid C# lambda expressions prefixed with a "return" statement.
These are compiled at runtime into delegate methods.

Correct format for filter expressions:
- "return (parameter) => boolean_expression;"

Correct format for cost expressions:
- "return (parameter) => numeric_expression;"

Sample request:

    POST /path/1/to/5
    {
       "pathAlgorithmName": "BLS",
       "maxDepth": 5,
       "maxPathWeight": 100.0,
       "maxResults": 10,
       "filter": {
         "vertexFilter": "return (v) => v.Label == \"Person\";",
         "edgeFilter": "return (e) => e.Label == \"friendship\";",
         "edgePropertyFilter": "return (p) => p == \"knows\";"
       },
       "cost": {
         "vertexCost": "return (v) => v.TryGetProperty(out var age, \"age\") ? (double)age : 1.0;",
         "edgeCost": "return (e) => e.TryGetProperty(out var weight, \"weight\") ? (double)weight : 1.0;"
       }
    }

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The ID of the source vertex

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The ID of the target vertex

requestBody.description: Path specification with algorithm, depth, filters and other constraints

200.description: Returns the found paths between the vertices

400.description: Invalid path specification, a fragment failed to compile, storedQuery was mixed with inline fragments, or the referenced stored query has the wrong kind

403.description: The request carries inline filter/cost fragments and dynamic code execution is disabled on this server (Fallen8:Security:EnableDynamicCodeExecution). Requests referencing a storedQuery - or carrying no fragments at all - are NOT gated by that switch.

404.description: Source or target vertex not found, or no stored query with the referenced name exists

409.description: The referenced stored query is not invocable (its recompile on load failed - see its diagnostics via GET /storedquery/{name})

delete.summary: Removes a graph element from an index

200.description: Returns true if the element was successfully removed from the index

404.description: Index not found or graph element not found

200.description: OK

put.summary: Loads a registered save game, replacing the current in-memory graph

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The save-game id

parameters.name: waitForCompletion
parameters.description: Wait for the load transaction to finish before responding

200.description: Loaded (waited); returns the save game

404.description: No save game with that id

500.description: The load transaction was rolled back

get.summary: Returns a graph-shape snapshot: counts, cardinalities, degrees, indices, memory
get.description:
The result is an ADVISORY snapshot, not transactionally consistent: reads are
lock-free over the volatile element snapshot, so a write committed during the pass
may or may not appear.

COST (honest): counts and memory are O(1); labels and property keys are one pass
over the element snapshot; degrees are O(1) adjacency-count reads per vertex. When
V+E exceeds the configured element budget (Fallen8:Observability:StatisticsElementBudget,
default 1,000,000) the pass samples with a uniform stride and the response says so:
sampled=true, sampleStride, per-name counts as counted IN THE SAMPLE (multiply by
the stride to extrapolate), distinctTotal = distinct within the sample (sampling
honestly undercounts distinct values). Degree percentiles from a strided sample are
statistically sound. Memory numbers never force a GC.

This endpoint exposes SCHEMA-SHAPED data (label names, property keys, index names),
so it sits behind the normal API-key policy - unlike /metrics, whose inventory is
aggregate numbers only - and under the sensitive rate limiter so a misconfigured
scrape loop cannot turn an O(V+E) pass into a DoS.

200.description: The snapshot

post.summary: Registers a stored query: validates and compiles the specification, then publishes it under a unique name.

requestBody.description: The stored query specification (name, kind, and the matching path/subGraph block)

201.description: The stored query was compiled and registered

400.description: The specification was malformed (name/kind/block), or a fragment failed to compile (the body carries the compiler diagnostics)

403.description: Dynamic code execution is disabled on this server (Fallen8:Security:EnableDynamicCodeExecution) - registration introduces code and is always gated

409.description: A stored query with the same name already exists, or the library quota (Fallen8:StoredQueries:MaxCount) was reached

500.description: The registration transaction faulted with an internal error

get.summary: Lists all registered stored queries.

200.description: Returns the stored query summaries

get.summary: Gets the full definition of a stored query, including its source specification.
get.description:
The response includes the stored specification JSON (the registration request's
path/subGraph block), which also covers manual migration between instances, and - for
a Failed entry - the recompile diagnostics.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The stored query name

200.description: Returns the stored query detail

404.description: No stored query with the given name exists

delete.description:
Deletion drops the pinned compiled artifact so its collectible load context can unload
once in-flight invocations finish. NOT gated by the dynamic-code switch: removal
compiles nothing and must stay possible while the switch is off.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The stored query name

404.description: No stored query with the given name exists

500.description: The removal transaction was rolled back and did not complete

put.summary: Creates and registers a new subgraph from a specification.

parameters.name: fromSubGraph
parameters.description: Optional name of an existing subgraph to source this one from (creates a nested subgraph). When omitted, the subgraph is sourced from the whole graph.

requestBody.description: The subgraph specification (name, filters, patterns)

201.description: The subgraph was created and registered. A syntactically-valid pattern that matches nothing yields a registered EMPTY subgraph (201), identically whether the source graph is empty or populated.

403.description: The request carries inline filter/pattern fragments and dynamic code execution is disabled on this server (Fallen8:Security:EnableDynamicCodeExecution). Requests referencing a storedQuery are NOT gated by that switch.

400.description: The specification was invalid, the pattern was structurally invalid, a filter failed to compile, storedQuery was mixed with inline fragments, or the referenced stored query has the wrong kind

404.description: The source subgraph named by fromSubGraph does not exist, or no stored query with the referenced name exists

409.description: A subgraph with the same name already exists, a resource quota (subgraph count or materialized-element ceiling) was exceeded, or the referenced stored query is not invocable (its recompile on load failed)

500.description: The create transaction faulted with an internal error

get.summary: Lists the names of all registered subgraphs.

200.description: Returns the list of subgraph names

get.summary: Gets a summary (metadata and element counts) of a registered subgraph.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The subgraph name

200.description: Returns the subgraph summary

404.description: No subgraph with the given name exists

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The subgraph name

404.description: No subgraph with the given name exists

500.description: The removal transaction was rolled back and did not complete

get.summary: Gets the extracted contents (vertices and edges) of a registered subgraph.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The subgraph name

parameters.name: maxElements
parameters.description: Maximum number of vertices and edges to return (default 1000)

200.description: Returns the subgraph contents

404.description: No subgraph with the given name exists

post.summary: Recalculates a subgraph against the current state of its source graph.
post.description:
Only subgraphs created by an algorithm (with a stored source and plugin name)
can be recalculated; manually registered subgraphs cannot.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The subgraph name

200.description: Returns the recalculated subgraph summary

404.description: No subgraph with the given name exists

409.description: The subgraph exists but cannot be recalculated

id.description: The unique identifier of the graph element

creationDate.description: The date and time when the element was created

modificationDate.description: The date and time when the element was last modified

label.description: The type label of the graph element used for categorization

properties.description: The collection of properties (key-value pairs) associated with the graph element

AGraphElement.description: Abstract base class for all graph elements (vertices and edges)

converged.description:
Whether the iterative algorithm converged (single-pass algorithms: true).
    False with usable values when the iteration cap stopped the run.

budgetExhausted.description:
True when the time budget or cancellation stopped the run and the values
    are the last completed iteration's (partial relative to the requested work).

vertexCount.description: How many vertices were in scope for the run.

statistics.description:
Run-level aggregates: TriangleCount, ComponentCount, CommunityCount,
    degree Min/Max/Mean.

results.description: Top-K scored vertices (score algorithms only), best first.

partitions.description: Partition summaries (partition algorithms only), largest first.

writeBack.description: The write-back report; null when the request did not opt in.

AnalyticsResultREST.description:
One analytics run's response: run metadata, statistics, and the BOUNDED projection of
the per-vertex result (top-K scores or partition summaries - the full result set's
delivery vehicle is write-back).

vertexLabel.description:
Only vertices with exactly this label participate (induced-subgraph
    scoping); null = whole graph.

edgePropertyId.description:
Only edges in this adjacency group (edge-property-id) are traversed;
    null = all edges.

direction.description:
Edge-direction interpretation: "in", "out" or "both". Null selects the
    algorithm's default (PAGERANK: out; DEGREE and LABELPROPAGATION: both; WCC and
    TRIANGLECOUNT ignore direction).

maxIterations.description:
Iteration cap for iterative algorithms (PAGERANK default 100,
    LABELPROPAGATION default 20; ceiling 10000). Reaching the cap is a normal outcome:
    converged=false, values usable.

epsilon.description: PageRank convergence threshold (L1 delta); 0 selects the default 1e-6.

timeBudgetSeconds.description:
Wall-clock budget in seconds; null selects the configured default (30 s).
    Values above the configured ceiling are a 400.

parameters.description: Algorithm-specific numeric knobs, e.g. {"DampingFactor": 0.85}.

maxResults.description:
How many rows the response carries (top-K scores or partition summaries).
    Default 100, ceiling 10000 - the full result set's delivery vehicle is write-back,
    not pagination through millions.

offset.description: Offset into a partition's membership page (partition endpoint only).

writeBack.description:
Opt-in: write each in-scope vertex's value as a property through chunked
    plugin write transactions. Snapshot-durable only (a WAL-only replay loses the
    properties; re-run to restore) - the mode-(a) contract of DelegateTransaction.

writeBackPropertyKey.description:
Overrides the convention property key (e.g. "analytics.pagerank");
    non-empty, at most 256 chars.

AnalyticsSpecification.description:
One analytics run request (feature graph-analytics): data-only scoping, budgets,
algorithm parameters, the bounded-result knob and the opt-in property write-back.
No dynamic code anywhere - these endpoints work with the dynamic-code switch off.

iterations.description: Number of timed iterations the statistics are computed over

edgesTraversed.description: Edges traversed in a single iteration

averageTps.description: Mean traversals per second across iterations

medianTps.description: Median traversals per second across iterations

standardDeviationTps.description: Standard deviation of the per-iteration TPS samples

BenchmarkResultREST.description: Structured result of the edge-traversal benchmark (GET /benchmark)

linesRead.description: The number of lines read from the request body (including meta and blank lines).

BulkImportResultREST.description: The success summary of a bulk JSONL import (feature bulk-import-export).

distinctTotal.description: Distinct names seen (within the sample when sampled=true).

CardinalityStatsREST.description:
Top-N names by count plus the distinct total. Sampling honestly UNDERCOUNTS
    distinct values: when sampled=true, distinctTotal is distinct-within-the-sample - the
    DTO documents that rather than pretending to estimate.

DegreeStatsREST.description:
Degree distribution over the sampled vertices - strided-sample percentiles are
    statistically sound.

id.description: The diagnostic id, e.g. "CS1061" (or "F8LIMIT" for the size guard)

DelegateDiagnosticREST.description: A single compiler diagnostic in fragment coordinates (feature web-ui, gap G-2)

valid.description: True when the fragment compiles without errors (warnings do not block)

diagnostics.description: All non-hidden compiler diagnostics, in fragment coordinates

DelegateValidationREST.description: Result of compile-checking a delegate fragment (feature web-ui, gap G-2)

targetVertex.description: The identifier of the vertex where this edge ends

sourceVertex.description: The identifier of the vertex where this edge starts

id.description: The unique identifier of the graph element

creationDate.description: The date and time when the element was created

modificationDate.description: The date and time when the element was last modified

label.description: The type label of the graph element used for categorization

properties.description: The collection of properties (key-value pairs) associated with the graph element

Edge.description: Represents an edge (relationship) between two vertices in the graph

creationDate.description: The creation date of the edge as a Unix timestamp

sourceVertex.description: The identifier of the source vertex where the edge starts

targetVertex.description: The identifier of the target vertex where the edge ends

edgePropertyId.description: The edge property identifier that defines the relationship type

properties.description: The properties of the edge as key-value pairs

label.description: The label of the edge used for categorization

EdgeSpecification.description: Specification for creating a new edge between two vertices in the graph

model.description:
The model-identity stamp written by the embedding provider, or null for a
    bring-your-own-vector embedding.

enabled.description: Whether the capability flag (Fallen8:Embedding:Enabled) is on.

backend.description: The configured backend: Onnx, LLamaSharp or Ollama.

modelVersion.description: The declared model version (empty when unspecified).

intendedMetric.description: The metric the embeddings are intended for.

loaded.description: Whether the backend has actually been created (lazy load happened).

EmbeddingProviderStatsREST.description:
The active embedding provider and its declared model identity (feature
    embedding-provider). Cheap config/state reads only - statistics never loads a model.

text.description: The query text (embedded once, with the configured query prefix).

k.description: How many nearest neighbours to return (1..1024).

kind.description: Optional element-kind constraint: vertex, edge, or any (default).

EmbeddingSearchSpecification.description:
Semantic search: embed a query text and run kNN against a vector index
    (feature embedding-provider).

vectors.description: One vector per input text, in order.

EmbeddingVectorsREST.description: Raw embedding vectors plus the identity they came from.

vector.description: The embedding vector; finite components, dimension within [1, 4096].

EmbeddingWriteSpecification.description: Sets (replace semantics) a named element embedding (feature element-embeddings).

EmbedElementItem.description: One item of a batch embed (feature embedding-provider).

EmbedElementSpecification.description:
Embed a text and store it as the element's named embedding (feature
    embedding-provider).

name.description: The embedding name for the whole batch (default "default").

EmbedElementsSpecification.description:
Embed a batch of texts onto elements in one provider batch and one
    transaction (feature embedding-provider) - the bulk-ingestion path.

texts.description: The texts to embed (bounded by Fallen8:Embedding:MaxBatchSize).

EmbedTextSpecification.description:
Embed raw texts and return the vectors (feature embedding-provider) - for
    clients that drive the raw vector surfaces themselves.

indexId.description: The identifier of the fulltext index to search

requestString.description: The search query text to look for in the indexed content

FulltextIndexScanSpecification.description: Specification for performing fulltext search queries on indexed content

graphElementId.description: The identifier of the graph element (vertex or edge) that matched the search query

highlights.description: Collection of highlighted text fragments showing the context of search term matches

score.description: The relevance score of this result, indicating how well it matches the search query

FulltextSearchResultElementREST.description: Represents a single match in fulltext search results with relevance scoring and highlighting

maximumScore.description: The highest relevance score among all matching elements

elements.description: Collection of matching elements with their relevance scores and highlighted content

FulltextSearchResultREST.description: Result container for fulltext search operations with scoring and highlighting

edges.description: Collection of all edges in the graph

vertices.description: Collection of all vertices in the graph

Graph.description: Represents an entire graph structure with vertices and edges

inDegree.description: In-degree distribution over the sampled vertices.

outDegree.description: Out-degree distribution over the sampled vertices.

totalDegree.description: Total (in+out) degree distribution over the sampled vertices.

propertyKeys.description:
Property-key cardinalities by element count (top-N + distinct) - the
    heaviest stat (O(total properties) over the sampled elements).

memory.description: Free process/GC memory reads (never a forced GC).

computedInMs.description: Wall-clock cost of computing this snapshot.

sampled.description:
True when V+E exceeded the element budget and the pass sampled with a
    uniform stride; per-name counts are then within-sample.

sampleStride.description: The uniform stride used (1 when exact).

embedding.description:
The embedding provider state (feature embedding-provider). Reading this
    NEVER triggers the lazy model load.

GraphStatisticsREST.description:
The graph-shape snapshot behind GET /statistics (feature observability). An ADVISORY,
lock-free snapshot - not transactionally consistent - computed on demand under an
element budget: exact when V+E fits the budget, uniformly strided (and flagged) above it.

graphElementId.description: The identifier of the graph element to index

key.description: The key under which to index the graph element

IndexAddToSpecification.description: Specification for adding or updating a graph element in an index

indexId.description: The index id used by the scan and index endpoints.

pluginType.description: The index plugin type the index was created with.

embeddingName.description:
For a vector index BOUND to an element embedding (feature element-embeddings), the
embedding name it derives its projection from; `null` for an unbound (raw)
vector index and for every other family. Lets a client show that the index is a
self-maintained projection and that explicit adds are rejected. Not captured in
save-game KPIs (only the live `/status` inventory populates it).

model.description:
For a vector index, the declared model-identity string it expects its vectors to
come from (feature embedding-provider), or `null`. Diagnostic only.

IndexDescriptionREST.description:
A registered index: its id and the index plugin type. Appears on
StatusREST (live inventory) and in save-game KPIs (inventory at save time).

indexId.description: The identifier of the index to scan

operator.description: The binary operator to use for comparing property values

literal.description: The literal value to compare against

label.description: Optional label filter to restrict the scan to specific element types

resultType.description: Specifies which types of graph elements to include in the results

IndexScanSpecification.description: Specification for scanning an index for elements with specific property values

value.description: The string representation of the value to use in comparisons

fullQualifiedTypeName.description: The fully qualified .NET type name of the value

LiteralSpecification.description: Specification for literal values used in graph queries and scans

startServices.description: Indicates whether services should be automatically started after loading

saveGameLocation.description: The file path location of the Fallen-8 database save file

LoadSpecification.description: Specification for loading a Fallen-8 database from disk

gcHeapBytes.description: GC.GetTotalMemory(false) - allocated managed memory, no forced collection.

MemoryStatsREST.description:
Process/GC memory numbers that are FREE to read - this endpoint never forces a
    GC (deliberate contrast with the benchmark-only GC.GetTotalMemory(true)).

count.description: How many sampled elements carry it.

NamedCountREST.description:
One label (or property key) with its element count. When the response is
    sampled, counts are AS COUNTED IN THE SAMPLE (multiply by sampleStride to extrapolate).

size.description: The partition's total size (across all pages).

offset.description: This page's offset into the ascending-id member list.

members.description: The member vertex ids on this page, ascending.

partitionId.description:
The partition id (WCC: smallest member vertex id; LABELPROPAGATION: the
    community's label, itself a vertex id).

size.description: The number of vertices in the partition.

PartitionSummaryREST.description:
One partition summary (partition algorithms), rows ordered size-descending
    with ascending-partition-id tie-break.

vertexCost.description: Cost function for vertices during path traversal

edgeCost.description: Cost function for edges during path traversal

PathCostSpecification.description: Specification for defining cost functions used in path calculations

sourceVertexId.description: The identifier of the vertex where this path segment begins

targetVertexId.description: The identifier of the vertex where this path segment ends

edgeId.description: The identifier of the edge connecting the source and target vertices

edgePropertyId.description: The property identifier/type of the edge in this path segment

direction.description: The direction in which the edge is traversed (OutgoingEdge, IncomingEdge, or UndirectedEdge)

weight.description: The cost/weight associated with traversing this path segment

PathElementREST.description: Represents a single segment of a path between vertices in the graph

edgePropertyFilter.description: Filter to apply on edge properties during path traversal

vertexFilter.description: Filter to apply on vertices during path traversal

edgeFilter.description: Filter to apply on edges during path traversal

PathFilterSpecification.description: Specification for filtering graph elements during path finding operations

pathElements.description: The ordered sequence of path elements (vertices and edges) that form the path

totalWeight.description: The aggregate weight/cost of the entire path

PathREST.description: Represents a path between two vertices in the graph

pathAlgorithmName.description: The algorithm to use for path finding.

maxDepth.description: The maximum number of edges in paths to consider

maxResults.description: The maximum number of paths to return in the result

maxPathWeight.description: The maximum allowed weight for a path to be included in results

filter.description: Filtering criteria for elements to include in path calculations

cost.description: Cost function specifications for weighting paths

storedQuery.description:
The name of a registered stored query of kind `Path` to use instead of inline
PathFilterSpecification PathSpecification.Filter/PathCostSpecification PathSpecification.Cost fragments (feature stored-query-library).
Mutually exclusive with them (400 when mixed). A stored-query request compiles
nothing and works with dynamic code execution disabled; the numeric bounds and
string PathSpecification.PathAlgorithmName stay per-request.

semantic.description:
The declarative semantic block (feature element-embeddings): the query vector for
the traversal context plus optional code-free similarity filter/cost. Pure data -
never gated by the dynamic-code switch, and deliberately NOT part of the compile
cache key (the context is a factory parameter, not source).

PathSpecification.description: Specification for finding paths between vertices in the graph

patternName.description: Optional name used to identify this pattern element in the definition.

graphElementFilter.description:
Filter applied to any graph element (vertex or edge). The lambda receives an
`AGraphElementModel`.

vertexFilter.description:
Vertex-specific filter. Only meaningful when string PatternSpecification.Type is
`Vertex`. The lambda receives a `VertexModel`.

direction.description:
Traversal direction for edge patterns: `OutgoingEdge`, `IncomingEdge`
or `UndirectedEdge`. Only meaningful for `Edge` /
`VariableLengthEdge`. Defaults to `OutgoingEdge`.

edgePropertyFilter.description:
Filter applied to an edge's property id (a string) before the edge itself is
inspected. Only meaningful for edge patterns.

edgeFilter.description:
Edge-specific filter. Only meaningful for edge patterns. The lambda receives
an `EdgeModel`.

minLength.description: Minimum number of hops for a `VariableLengthEdge` pattern.

maxLength.description: Maximum number of hops for a `VariableLengthEdge` pattern.

PatternSpecification.description: A single element of a subgraph pattern sequence.

uniqueId.description: A unique identifier for this plugin instance

pluginType.description: The name of the plugin type to instantiate

pluginOptions.description: Configuration options for the plugin as key-value pairs

PluginSpecification.description: Specification for creating and configuring a plugin service

propertyId.description: The identifier name of the property

fullQualifiedTypeName.description: The fully qualified .NET type name of the property

propertyValue.description: The string representation of the property value

PropertySpecification.description: Defines a property with name, type, and value for plugin configuration

indexId.description: The identifier of the index to scan

leftLimit.description: The lower bound of the range as a string representation

rightLimit.description: The upper bound of the range as a string representation

fullQualifiedTypeName.description: The fully qualified .NET type name of the range values

includeLeft.description: Whether to include the lower bound value in the range (inclusive)

includeRight.description: Whether to include the upper bound value in the range (inclusive)

resultType.description: Specifies which types of graph elements to include in the results

RangeIndexScanSpecification.description: Specification for performing a range-based scan on an index

ResultTypeSpecification.description: Specifies which types of graph elements to include in query results

SaveGameKpisREST.description: Cheap KPIs captured at save/import time - values the engine already has, no graph scan.

id.description: Stable id; sortable timestamp prefix + random suffix. The REST identifier.

savedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC instant the save game was created (or the file's write time for imports).

trigger.description: How the entry was created: "api", "shutdown" or "imported".

location.description: Absolute path of the primary checkpoint file.

fileCount.description: Number of files belonging to this save game (checkpoint + partitions + sidecars).

totalBytes.description: Total size of those files in bytes at registration time.

engineVersion.description: The engine/assembly version that produced the entry.

SaveGameREST.description: A registered save game (checkpoint) with its metadata (feature save-games).

saveGameLocation.description: The file path location where the Fallen-8 database should be saved

savePartitions.description: The number of partitions to use when saving (optional, defaults to optimal based on processor count)

SaveSpecification.description: Specification for saving a Fallen-8 database to disk

operator.description: The binary operator to use for comparing property values

literal.description: The literal value to compare against

label.description: Optional label filter to restrict the scan to specific element types

resultType.description: Specifies which types of graph elements to include in the results

ScanSpecification.description: Specification for scanning the graph for elements with specific property values

score.description: The raw score (PageRank mass, degree, triangle count).

ScoredVertexREST.description:
One scored vertex (score algorithms), rows ordered score-descending with
    ascending-id tie-break.

indexId.description: The identifier of the spatial index to search

graphElementId.description: The identifier of the reference graph element for the distance calculation

distance.description: The maximum distance to search within

SearchDistanceSpecification.description: Specification for performing spatial distance searches in the graph

queryVector.description: The query vector to score elements against.

queryText.description:
A query TEXT to embed instead of supplying float[] SemanticTraversalSpecification.QueryVector (feature
embedding-provider; mutually exclusive with it). Embedded ONCE, before the
traversal starts, by the active provider - requires the EmbeddingProvider
capability (403 when `Fallen8:Embedding:Enabled` is off).

metric.description: The metric: Cosine (default), DotProduct or L2.

minScore.description:
Optional declarative filter threshold: an element passes when its named embedding
scores at least this well (at most, under L2); elements without the embedding are
filtered.

costBySimilarity.description:
Optional declarative vertex cost (path requests only): Cosine maps to
`1 - score`, L2 to the distance itself; DotProduct has no honest non-negative
mapping and is rejected. Vertices without the embedding are filtered.

SemanticTraversalSpecification.description:
The declarative semantic block of a path or subgraph request (feature
element-embeddings). Carries the query vector for the traversal context - embedded
ONCE, before the traversal starts - plus optional code-free filter/cost derivations.
Pure data, so it needs no dynamic-code capability; conflicts with a C# fragment or a
stored query owning the same delegate slot are rejected (one owner per slot).

usedMemory.description: The amount of memory currently used by the database process in bytes

vertexCount.description: The total number of vertices in the database

edgeCount.description: The total number of edges in the database

indices.description:
The indices currently registered on this instance (id + plugin type) — the live
inventory, available without running the budgeted statistics pass

availableIndexPlugins.description: List of available index plugins that can be used with the database

availablePathPlugins.description: List of available path-finding algorithm plugins

availableAnalyticsPlugins.description: List of available graph-analytics algorithm plugins

availableServicePlugins.description: List of available service plugins that can be started with the database

apiKeyRequired.description:
True when this server has an API key configured, i.e. every endpoint outside the
anonymous allowlist answers 401 without a valid credential. /status itself stays
anonymous, so it doubles as the connection probe: a caller is authorized iff
`!ApiKeyRequired || Authenticated`.

authenticated.description:
True when the request that produced this status carried a valid credential
(see bool StatusREST.ApiKeyRequired for how clients combine the two).

StatusREST.description: Represents the current status of the Fallen-8 database

filter.description: Filtering criteria for elements to include in path calculations.

cost.description: Cost function specifications for weighting paths.

StoredPathQueryBlock.description:
The stored form of a path query: the `filter`/`cost` blocks of a
PathSpecification. The numeric bounds (`maxDepth`, `maxResults`,
`maxPathWeight`) and the algorithm name stay per-request.

specificationJson.description:
The stored specification document (JSON text): the registration request's
`path` / `subGraph` block exactly as stored.

compileDiagnostics.description:
The compiler diagnostics of a failed rehydration recompile; null unless
string StoredQuerySummaryREST.CompileState is `Failed`.

compileState.description:
The compile state: `Compiled` (invocable), `Failed` (a rehydration
recompile failed - see the detail response's diagnostics; invoking returns 409), or
`SourceOnly` (loaded without a compiler).

StoredQueryDetailREST.description:
Full detail of a registered stored query, INCLUDING its source specification - the
library is transparent to its operator (and `GET` covers manual migration between
instances).

name.description:
The unique name to register the query under. Restricted to
`^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,128}$` (always a safe URL path segment); compared
case-sensitively.

kind.description:
The stored query kind: `Path` (a filter/cost set for `POST /path`) or
`SubGraph` (a pattern template for `PUT /subgraph`).

path.description: The path filter/cost block (required iff string StoredQuerySpecification.Kind is `Path`).

subGraph.description:
The subgraph pattern template block (required iff string StoredQuerySpecification.Kind is
`SubGraph`). The subgraph instance name and additional information stay
per-request on `PUT /subgraph` and are not part of the stored template.

StoredQuerySpecification.description:
Specification for registering a stored query (feature stored-query-library): a named,
validated, pre-compiled query definition that the path/subgraph endpoints can afterwards
reference by name - including when dynamic code execution is disabled.

compileState.description:
The compile state: `Compiled` (invocable), `Failed` (a rehydration
recompile failed - see the detail response's diagnostics; invoking returns 409), or
`SourceOnly` (loaded without a compiler).

StoredQuerySummaryREST.description: Summary of a registered stored query (list/detail responses).

vertexFilter.description:
Optional pre-filter selecting which vertices are copied into the subgraph
(a C# fragment receiving an `AGraphElementModel`).

edgeFilter.description:
Optional pre-filter selecting which edges are copied into the subgraph
(a C# fragment receiving an `AGraphElementModel`).

patterns.description:
Ordered pattern sequence describing the paths to keep (see
List<PatternSpecification> SubGraphSpecification.Patterns).

StoredSubGraphQueryBlock.description:
The stored form of a subgraph query: a SubGraphSpecification WITHOUT the
per-instance `name`/`additionalInformation` fields.

name.description: Unique name the subgraph is registered under.

additionalInformation.description: Optional metadata attached to the subgraph definition.

vertexFilter.description:
Optional pre-filter selecting which vertices are copied into the subgraph.
Null or empty copies all vertices. The lambda receives an
`AGraphElementModel`.

edgeFilter.description:
Optional pre-filter selecting which edges are copied into the subgraph. Null
or empty copies all edges whose endpoints were copied. The lambda receives an
`AGraphElementModel`.

patterns.description:
Ordered pattern sequence describing the paths to keep. Should alternate
vertex ↔ edge and start with a vertex pattern. Empty means "no pruning".

storedQuery.description:
The name of a registered stored query of kind `SubGraph` to instantiate instead
of inline string SubGraphSpecification.VertexFilter/string SubGraphSpecification.EdgeFilter/List<PatternSpecification> SubGraphSpecification.Patterns
fragments (feature stored-query-library). Mutually exclusive with them (400 when
mixed); string SubGraphSpecification.Name (and optional Dictionary<string, string> SubGraphSpecification.AdditionalInformation) stay
required per instance. A stored-query request compiles nothing and works with
dynamic code execution disabled.

semantic.description:
The declarative semantic block (feature element-embeddings): the query vector the
compiled filters' traversal context carries, plus an optional code-free
`minScore` vertex pre-filter. Bound at REGISTRATION time - recalculation
reuses the same delegates and never embeds anything. Pure data (not gated by the
dynamic-code switch); mutually exclusive with string SubGraphSpecification.StoredQuery and, when
`minScore` is set, with an inline string SubGraphSpecification.VertexFilter fragment.

SubGraphSpecification.description: Specification for creating a subgraph by pattern matching against the graph.

name.description: The name the subgraph is registered under.

vertexCount.description: Number of vertices in the extracted subgraph.

edgeCount.description: Number of edges in the extracted subgraph.

algorithmPluginName.description: The algorithm plugin used to create the subgraph.

sourceFallen8Id.description: The id of the source graph the subgraph was created from.

canRecalculate.description: Whether this subgraph can be recalculated against its source.

additionalInformation.description: Metadata attached to the subgraph definition.

SubGraphSummary.description:
A lightweight summary of a registered subgraph (metadata and element counts),
without the full vertex/edge payload.

delegateKind.description: The delegate kind the fragment must compile against

fragment.description: The C# fragment to validate: a method body returning a lambda

ValidateDelegateSpecification.description: Request to compile-check a single delegate fragment (feature web-ui, gap G-2)

vector.description: The embedding vector (explicit mode). Must match the index dimension.

propertyId.description:
The element property holding the vector (property mode); the property must
    be a float[] of the index dimension.

VectorIndexAddSpecification.description:
Adds (or replaces) an element's vector in a vector index (feature vector-index).
Exactly one mode: an explicit float[] VectorIndexAddSpecification.Vector, or string VectorIndexAddSpecification.PropertyId naming
a `float[]` property on the element to read the vector from.

query.description: The query vector; must match the index dimension.

k.description: How many nearest neighbours to return (1..1024).

kind.description: Optional element-kind constraint: vertex, edge, or any (default).

label.description:
Optional exact (case-sensitive) label constraint; an unlabeled element never
    matches.

VectorIndexScanSpecification.description: A k-nearest-neighbour query against a vector index (feature vector-index).

score.description: The raw score (no normalization; interpret via metric/higherIsBetter).

VectorScoredElementREST.description: One kNN hit: the element id and its RAW score under the index metric.

metric.description: The index's metric: Cosine, DotProduct or L2.

higherIsBetter.description: Whether a HIGHER score is better (false for L2).

results.description: The hits, best first; ties broken by ascending element id.

VectorSearchResultREST.description:
A kNN result: hits best-first plus the metric and its direction, so an L2 distance can
never be misread as a similarity.

outEdges.description: Dictionary of outgoing edges grouped by edge property type

inEdges.description: Dictionary of incoming edges grouped by edge property type

id.description: The unique identifier of the graph element

creationDate.description: The date and time when the element was created

modificationDate.description: The date and time when the element was last modified

label.description: The type label of the graph element used for categorization

properties.description: The collection of properties (key-value pairs) associated with the graph element

Vertex.description: Represents a vertex (node) in the graph with its properties and connected edges

creationDate.description: The creation date of the vertex as a Unix timestamp

label.description: The label of the vertex used for categorization

properties.description: The properties of the vertex as key-value pairs

VertexSpecification.description: Specification for creating a new vertex in the graph

propertyKey.description: The property key the values were written under.

verticesWritten.description:
How many vertex writes the committed chunks carried. A vertex removed
    concurrently between the run and its chunk is a silent no-op on the writer yet
    still counted - the fuzzy-consistency story applies to write-back too.

chunks.description:
How many DelegateTransaction chunks carried the write-back (each chunk is
    atomic; the whole write-back is not - re-run to remedy a mid-way failure).

WriteBackResultREST.description: The write-back report when the request opted in.
